mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises refinancing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 4.94% exceeds restrictive threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50,000 persons in single month
regime_hint: labor market contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite payroll decline, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in real retail and food services sales signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when RSAFS declines > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO months do not produce higher average 5-day returns than negative INDPRO months (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and retail sales signals synchronized demand collapse across both residential and consumer sectors, amplifying negative equity risk premium and producing sharply negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 8% and RSAFS declines > 1.2% in same month
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock or early recession
failure_condition: Joint deterioration events produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction effect (measured as coefficient on HOUST×RSAFS interaction term) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive regression.

mechanism_narrative: Combination of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 210 index points) and restrictive federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals sustained real rate tightening that compresses equity valuations and reduces real asset returns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient between real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation rate) and 60-day returns is not significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rise above 5.0% signals labor market slack and reduced wage growth expectations, which compresses corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but only when the rise occurs within a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25pp in 1 month AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect between UNRATE rise and FEDFUNDS level is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained contraction in real GDP growth (GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5%) signals late-cycle or recessionary demand weakness that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11,520.1 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
